_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 8-2 Patient Prioritization and Lights and Sirens (Revised 3/31/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
condition: Patient Prioritization and Lights and Sirens
protocol_reference: Michigan System Protocol Section 8-2: Patient Prioritization and Lights and Sirens — MDHHS Approved 3/31/2025
purpose: Govern EMS patient prioritization and use of emergency lights and sirens (L&S) during response and transport. L&S use increases risk to crew, patient, and public — L&S must be clinically justified. Time savings from L&S use are minimal; patient benefit must outweigh the risk.

sections.title: Priority Levels
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-2: Patient Prioritization — Priority Levels
points: PRIORITY 1 (EMERGENT): Life-threatening condition — immediate intervention required; L&S response and transport indicated
PRIORITY 2 (URGENT): Serious but not immediately life-threatening — may benefit from expedited response; L&S at crew discretion
PRIORITY 3 (NON-EMERGENT): Stable, no time-sensitive intervention required — routine response and transport; no L&S
DEAD ON SCENE / DOA: No L&S transport required

sections.title: Conditions Typically Warranting Priority 1 (L&S Transport)
points: Cardiac arrest (including post-ROSC transport when indicated)
Active STEMI with cath lab activation
Respiratory failure or arrest
Airway compromise
Altered mental status with hemodynamic instability
Stroke with large vessel occlusion or hemorrhagic presentation
Major trauma with hemodynamic instability
Obstetric emergency (active delivery, prolapsed cord, eclampsia)
Septic shock
Anaphylaxis with hemodynamic compromise
Status epilepticus
Pediatric patients with critical illness or injury

sections.title: L&S Use — Key Principles
points: L&S use is a clinical decision — the crew chief is responsible for determining whether L&S are indicated
L&S do not guarantee faster arrival and carry inherent risk — use only when patient benefit is expected
Routine transport of stable patients does NOT warrant L&S — L&S transport for stable patients increases risk without benefit
MCAs may define specific conditions that require or prohibit L&S transport
Patient or family preference alone is NOT sufficient justification for L&S transport
All L&S transport decisions must be documented in the PCR with clinical justification

sections.title: L&S Use Considerations
points: Consider road conditions, traffic, and weather when determining benefit of L&S
Consider proximity to receiving facility — very short transport distances reduce benefit of L&S
Notify receiving facility of incoming Priority 1 patient
Use of L&S does not change standard of care — patient assessment and treatment continue regardless of transport mode
Transport without L&S is appropriate for stable post-cardiac arrest patients without hemodynamic compromise at crew chief discretion

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may define specific conditions requiring or prohibiting L&S transport in their jurisdiction

deterioration_flags: Patient condition deteriorates during non-L&S transport — upgrade to L&S; notify receiving facility
Crew safety concern during L&S response — downgrade to routine; reassess L&S need on scene
scope_notes: L&S is a clinical decision by the crew chief
Priority 1 conditions: cardiac arrest, STEMI, respiratory failure, stroke, major trauma, OB emergency, septic shock, anaphylaxis, status epilepticus, critical pediatric
Stable patients do NOT warrant L&S transport
Document L&S use decision and clinical justification in PCR
Patient/family preference alone does not justify L&S
